design_philosophy.core_loop: Combat-focused beat 'em up with character-driven action sequences; chain attacks and manage positioning against waves of enemies and boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Arcade-style combat execution versus enemy patterns; survival through multiple stages with escalating difficulty
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Superhero power fantasy—playing as a martial artist fighter (Sonic Blast Man, Sonia, or Captain Choyear) punching through obstacles and defeating criminal/enemy bosses
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Direct spiritual successor to arcade Sonic Blast Man with expanded roster and multiplayer options; leans into arcade sensibilities on home console, valuing combat feedback and character variety over narrative depth

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple playable characters with different movesets provide mechanical variety; two-player mode suggests scaling difficulty options

progression.unlock_mechanism: Character roster expansion—additional playable characters (Sonia, Captain Choyear) available beyond original protagonist
replayability_hooks: Multiple playable characters with distinct combat styles
Character-specific stage variations (Stage 4 boss changes by character/region)

world_building_techniques: Character-driven protagonist (regional localization: 'I FIGHT FOR FREEDOM!' in NA version)
Boss character design reflects narrative stakes—including civilian/criminal character types
Regional narrative differences suggest thematic adaptation

boss_design.philosophy: Arcade-inspired boss encounters featuring distinctive character designs; bosses appear to represent criminal/enemy archetypes within the game's superhero narrative
notable_bosses: Stage 4 boss: Unmasked female with long hair (Japanese version) / Masked male with short hair (North American version)—indicates region-specific narrative choices around boss characterization

faction_indicators: Hero vs. Criminal dynamic (Sonic Blast Man as vigilante/superhero fighting crime)
Playable character roster variation suggests multiple faction perspectives within same narrative
danger_signals: Combat-centric design emphasizes fighting mechanical superiority over dialogue
Regional censorship of boss design (gender/appearance changes) suggests content sensitivity
environmental_storytelling: Boss character design carries narrative weight—appearance and presentation communicate threat level and character role

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Sonic Blast Man II
